text:
There once was a small house that had a secret. It had a tiny door, always just big enough for a small mouse. One bright spring morning, a little old lady mouse peered through the mouse hole.

"Good morning world," she chirped, then paused. It was just a regular old chair mouse with a peculiarly crooked tail.

"You go first!" said the chattering old lady mouse.

The little mouse hopped over and fluttered through the tiny door. There sat the chair mouse, sipping tea.

The little lady mouse hopped over and said, "You're adorable. I have tea just like that."

They chatted about everything and everything under the sun until tea time finally came.

Afterward, just as sweet tea glows in the evening light, the cozy little mouse house hummed softly.

The lady mouse hopped upstairs to bed. Each starlit room whispered the adventures she dreamt.
